tr.td: Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (2.5 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Hydrocortisone 10 mg/g and urea 100 mg/g cutaneous cream
Apply a thin layer (approximately 1 g) to the affected area twice daily
Doxycycline (as doxycycline hyclate) 100 mg oral capsule
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Live attenuated Human alphaherpesvirus 3 only vaccine product

ul.li: Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Achieve weight loss to support blood pressure management.
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week.
Patient will participate in a diabetes self-management education program to improve understanding of prediabetes and lifestyle modifications.
Patient will achieve a 5% reduction in body weight to decrease risk of progression to diabetes.
Patient will maintain hemoglobin A1c below 5.7% to prevent progression to diabetes.
Patient will engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week.

tr.td: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Erythrocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Hematocrit [Volume Fraction] of Blood by Automated count
Erythrocyte distribution width [Entitic volume] by Automated count
Platelets [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Platelet distribution width [Entitic volume] in Blood by Automated count
Platelet mean volume [Entitic volume] in Blood by Automated count

display.value: Disease caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (disorder)

ul.li: Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Achieve weight loss to support blood pressure management.
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week.
Patient will participate in a diabetes self-management education program to improve understanding of prediabetes and lifestyle modifications.
Patient will achieve a 5% reduction in body weight to decrease risk of progression to diabetes.
Patient will maintain hemoglobin A1c below 5.7% to prevent progression to diabetes.
Patient will engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week.
